game_name: Battletoads & Double Dragon: The Ultimate Team

danger_signals.visual_form: Shadow Warriors and Dark Queen forces on-screen
Shadow Boss and Dark Queen as distinct, larger sprite forms
danger_signals.meaning: Major escalation of danger; stage-culminating threats

danger_signals.visual_form: Futuristic metallic corridors, exterior ship hull, asteroid fields
danger_signals.meaning: Hostile alien/space terrain indicating high-stakes mission context

danger_signals.visual_form: Suspended cables, narrow ledges, hanging sequences
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard; player can lose life if enemies step on fingers or fall

safety_signals.visual_form: Five playable characters available (Zitz, Pimple, Rash, Billy, Jimmy)
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency and preparation before combat sections
Limited but defined recovery windows after failure

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Earth-based origin; receive mission briefing to defend Earth

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Billy and Jimmy Lee martial arts identity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Recruited as allied partners; integrate Dragon Force techniques into combat

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Dark Queen as primary antagonist; Shadow Warriors as foot soldiers
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Shadow Warriors appear as distinct enemy type distinct from generic forces
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Control Colossus spaceship; allied with Shadow Warriors; operate from outer space/Moon base

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character roster and briefing context (Dark Queen has fled, now returns with Colossus)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players select from five characters; preparation phase before engagement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Giant spaceship emerging from Moon; Earth's military neutralized (implied through context)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Battletoads contact Lee brothers for alliance; immediate deployment to Colossus tail

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual progression from exterior (ship tail) → interior corridors → ship base; metallic sci-fi environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat enemies increase in density; environmental hazards (ropes, platforms) escalate

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Game shifts to shooter perspective; Dark Queen throws asteroids, mines, UFOs at player spacecraft
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mechanic shift from beat 'em up to ranged/dodging gameplay; indicates space environment hostility

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Colossus successfully destroyed but Dark Queen escapes in missile hull; visual shift to chase sequence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must board escape vessel; mission objective escalates to direct confrontation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss-only stages; Shadow Boss first, then Dark Queen as final form
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Isolated arena battles; culminating threat visualization

environmental_storytelling.example: Level structure mirrors invasion logic: exterior ship tail → internal corridors → ship core → external destruction → escape pursuit → final bosses. Visuals show increasing penetration into enemy territory
Shadow Warriors appear throughout Colossus levels, visually reinforcing the alliance between Dark Queen and Double Dragon antagonists
Level 4 shifts to shooter genre when players attack spaceship from outside; visual and mechanical change communicates environmental transition from ground combat to space engagement
Five playable characters (three Battletoads + two Lee brothers) visually represent the unified team concept; roster selection reinforces narrative alliance

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Execute signature 'Smash Hit' attacks (Kiss-My-Fist, Big Bad Foot, Nuclear Knuckles, Battletoad Butt, BT Bashing Ball, Swingin' Size Thirteens, Take Out the Trash, Bikin' Bash, No Way Back Thwack, Twin Side Slam, Back 'N Front Punt)
Execute Dragon Force techniques (spin kick, knee drops, elbow-drops); differentiated moveset from toads
Appear as enemy forces throughout Colossus; serve as visual reinforcement of Dark Queen-Double Dragon antagonist alliance
Initially flees; returns with Colossus; survives ship destruction by escaping in missile hull; engages in final boss battle with full arsenal (asteroids, mines, UFOs in space phase)
Survives Colossus destruction with Dark Queen; serves as penultimate boss encounter before final confrontation
Can hang from cables/ledges; vulnerable to enemy interference (stepping on fingers); can fall and lose life; new mechanic indicating environmental hazard awareness

notes: This game primarily manifests world-state through spatial/environmental progression and mechanic shifts rather than persistent visual HUD elements. The crossover nature (Battletoads + Double Dragon) is communicated through playable roster and integrated attack movesets. Danger escalates through level structure (exterior → interior → core → space → chase → boss), with enemy variety (Shadow Warriors specifically) and environmental hazards (rope vulnerability) as threat indicators. Limited source material provides qualitative rather than quantitative visual data.
